page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t5-c1m2.xlarge is a burstable instance type powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with 1 thread per core, operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it operates on a KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates that the ecs.t5-c1m2.xlarge delivers entry-level performance. Single-core and multi-core CPU benchmarks, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, place this instance in the weak to poor performance categories. Memory bandwidth tests show weak read performance, though large-block write bandwidth reaches average levels. Cryptographic and compression tasks, including gzip and bzip3, also register in the lower percentiles, reflecting the burstable nature of the CPU allocation.

This instance is designed for qualitative cost efficiency, offering a budget-friendly profile for workloads that do not require continuous full CPU utilization. The primary tradeoff is the burstable CPU credit system; sustained high-intensity processing will deplete credits, leading to throttled performance. The lack of local storage requires external block storage attachment, which should be factored into the overall architectural design.

The ecs.t5-c1m2.xlarge is best suited for low-traffic web servers, microservices, development and testing environments, code repositories, and light database operations that experience periodic spikes in activity rather than sustained heavy loads.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t5-c1m2.xlarge is a burstable x86_64 instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 4 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 8.0 GB of RAM, yielding a 2.0 GB per core memory ratio. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, the instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks place this instance in the lower tiers, with weak single-core and multi-core CPU results and poor database operation metrics. However, its burstable CPU allocation provides a highly cost-effective option for workloads with low baseline utilization. It is best suited for development environments, low-traffic web serving, and light background tasks.
